_comment: Registry of managed AI tools. You can edit ports, python_version, torch index, and launch_env here. ${...} placeholders are filled in at runtime by server.py: ${venv_python}, ${python}, ${git}, ${port}, ${tool_dir}, ${clone_url}, ${branch}. A tool may also carry 'platform_overrides': {'windows': {...}, 'posix': {...}, 'mac': {...}, 'linux': {...}} — those keys replace the tool's top-level keys on that platform ('posix' covers mac+linux; the exact platform key wins over 'posix').

tools.name: ComfyUI
tools.description: Node-graph diffusion engine. Most flexible SD/SDXL/Flux backend. Web UI on port 8188.
tools.notes: ComfyUI-Manager is bundled in automatically (custom_nodes/comfyui-manager) — use the 'Manager' button in the ComfyUI UI to install custom nodes & models. Place model checkpoints in ComfyUI\models\checkpoints. torch installs from the CUDA 12.8 (cu128) index for RTX 50-series — uses --index-url (NOT --extra-index-url) so pip can't fall back to the CPU build on PyPI. For ComfyUI's newest optimized CUDA kernels you can switch the index to cu130; cu128 works fine otherwise.

posix.notes: Runs great on Apple Silicon — PyTorch uses Metal (MPS) automatically, installed straight from PyPI (no CUDA index needed). ComfyUI-Manager is bundled in automatically (custom_nodes/comfyui-manager) — use the 'Manager' button inside ComfyUI to add custom nodes & models. Place model checkpoints in ComfyUI/models/checkpoints.

tools.name: SD WebUI Forge
tools.description: A1111-style WebUI, optimized. Great all-rounder for SD/SDXL/Flux. Web UI on port 7860.
tools.notes: First launch builds its own venv and downloads ~6GB of torch/deps (several minutes). TORCH_COMMAND is overridden to cu128 for RTX 50-series; clear it if you are on an older GPU.

posix.notes: Runs via the repo's own webui.sh (it sources webui-macos-env.sh on Macs and sets itself up on first launch — several GB, be patient). Forge is CUDA-first: on Apple Silicon it runs on Metal (MPS) but is EXPERIMENTAL and slower — ComfyUI or Fooocus are the smoother choices on a Mac.

tools.name: Fooocus
tools.description: Simplest SDXL app — Midjourney-like, zero config. Auto-updates on launch. Web UI on port 7865.
tools.notes: First launch installs deps and downloads ~30GB of default SDXL models. entry_with_update.py self-updates (git pull) every launch. TORCH_COMMAND overridden to cu128 for RTX 50-series.

posix.notes: First launch installs deps and downloads ~30GB of default SDXL models. entry_with_update.py self-updates (git pull) every launch. On Apple Silicon, torch comes from PyPI and generates on Metal (MPS) — noticeably slower than an NVIDIA card, but it works. If model loading feels slow on an M2 or newer, add --disable-offload-from-vram to the run command.

tools.name: Kohya_ss
tools.description: LoRA / Dreambooth / fine-tune TRAINING GUI (not image generation). Web UI on port 7806.
tools.notes: Uses a git submodule (sd-scripts) — cloned with --recurse-submodules and updated with submodule update. Port set to 7806 to avoid clashing with Forge (7860). setup.bat builds its own venv.

posix.notes: Uses the repo's own setup.sh / gui.sh (they detect macOS and pick the Apple-Silicon requirements; Homebrew fills in any missing bits). Training is CUDA-first: on a Mac it is LIMITED and slow — fine for exploring the GUI, but serious LoRA training really wants an NVIDIA GPU. setup.sh builds its own venv.
